name: Australian Capital Territory (Australia)

colors.symbolism: the territorial colour at the hoist, taken from the Canberra city colours
the territorial colour at the fly, taken from the Canberra city colours
the stars of the Southern Cross and the swan and castle on the shield
the chief of the shield and details of the heraldic charge
the rose, mace and other heraldic elements on the shield

description: A vertical bicolour with a navy panel at the hoist (one third) bearing the white stars of the Southern Cross, and a yellow panel at the fly (two thirds) bearing the Canberra coat of arms.
history: Adopted on 25 March 1993, when the Australian Capital Territory's self-government had matured enough to warrant a distinct flag. The Canberra coat of arms used in the fly was granted by King George V in 1928, the year Canberra was officially designated the federal capital.

features.symbolism: blue and gold are the territorial colours, derived from the colours of the city of Canberra

features.role: the Southern Cross constellation on the blue panel
features.symbolism: the constellation Crux, visible in the southern sky

features.role: the Canberra coat of arms (shield) on the yellow panel
features.symbolism: the arms of the city of Canberra, granted in 1928, featuring the portcullis, sword, mace, rose, white swan and black swan, and a crown
